chosen_evolution_point.selection_rationale: The previous iteration used match_phase.cpp with Balanced Optimizer and achieved area improvement (+5.29%) but significant delay degradation (-82.14%). The adaptive signals show no recent improvements in either metric and recommend exploring match_drop_phase.cpp, but diversity pressure is LOW. However, match_phase_exact.cpp is better suited for precise area-delay trade-offs during exact optimization rounds using cut_ref/cut_deref for accurate area accounting. Since the current failure is delay degradation without area regression, targeting the exact phase allows us to enforce stricter timing constraints while maintaining area gains.

evolution_step.evolution_point_id: match_phase_exact comparison logic inside flag_compare_map calculation
evolution_step.objective: Prioritize cuts that meet timing constraints more aggressively even at slight area cost
evolution_step.direction_and_strategy: Replace the current timing-slack-aware comparison with a stricter required time check that rejects any cut violating node_data.required[phase] by more than epsilon, and prioritize cuts with better arrival times when area differences are within tolerance
evolution_step.expected_impact: 1-3% delay reduction, tolerate up to 2% area increase
evolution_step.constraints: Preserve API/templates; EVOLVE-BLOCK only; maintain complexity; keep epsilon compares
evolution_step.rationale: The current match_phase_exact implementation allows cuts with timing violations (only filtered by worst_arrival > required + epsilon) but its comparison logic doesn't sufficiently prioritize timing-critical paths. By strengthening the timing constraint enforcement in the selection criteria and making arrival time the primary discriminator when area is comparable, we can recover delay performance lost in the previous balanced optimization attempt. This aligns with the need to address the severe delay degradation while leveraging the exact area accounting mechanism unique to this module.
